name: set (from lodash)
description: A source-faithful extracted bundle of lodash's set method and its required helpers, cut from the full lodash package instead of the standalone lodash.set package.
tagline: Extract lodash set from the monolith with helpers included.
summary: This page ships an extracted bundle from the full lodash package. The point is not to use the modular lodash.set package, but to prove that a monolithic package can be reduced to one vendorable write-path behavior plus its helper chain.
nativeAlternative: Direct assignment handles shallow cases, but dynamic string paths and intermediate object creation make lodash-style set semantics much harder to replace safely.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization after extraction. The extraction defines the raw bundle; normalization only changes the packaging shape.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Source-faithful extracted bundle from the lodash package, with local helper modules inlined and module boundaries removed.

note: This snippet is the second monolith extraction experiment. It differs from the modular lodash.set package because the raw source is assembled from the full lodash package's internal module graph.
